LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM NG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE FOR THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT AS TO THE 4TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED. THE COURT CANNOT RULE AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THE CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT CLEARLY DISCLOSE THAT THE CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED AND THE DISCOVERY RULE MAY APPLY IN THIS CASE. FURTHER, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS NOT AVAILABLE TO PLAINTIFF AS TO DEFENDANTS' COUNTERCLAIM IF THE PERIOD HAS NOT RUN ON IT AT THE TIME OF THE COMMENCEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S ACTION EVEN THOUGH IT HAS RUN WHEN THE COUNTERCLAIM IS PLEADED. JONES V. MORTIMER (1946) 28 CAL. 2ND 627, 633. (DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM NG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE FOR THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST'S DEMURRER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, AND 10. APPEARANCES: GARY T. LEE, ESQ. 650/423-8888 FOR PLTF/X-DEFT & MOVING PARTY; EDWARD LIU, ESQ. 781-8251 FOR DEFT/X-COMPLAINANT & OPPOSING PARTY IS USTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 4TH-8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINT MUST ALLEGE FACTS DEMONSTRATING THAT THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE DECISION IN PARSONS V. TICKNER (1995) 31 CAL. APP. 4TH 1513, 1525 EXPLAINS WHAT IS REQUIRED. IN ADDITION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST DESCRIBE THE ASSETS THAT ALLEGEDLY WERE CONVERTED IN THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION; MUST PLEAD FRAUD WITH GREATER PARTICULARITY; AND MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR ELDER ABUSE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 9TH AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR THE FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT SUPPORT SUCH CLAIMS. IN ADDITION, COURT ALLOWS LEAVE TO AMEND UNTIL 12/5/08. ORDER ON DEMURRER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM NG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE FOR THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST Motion to Strike. Appearances: Gary Lee, Esq. Attorney at Law, (650) 423-8888, appearing for Plaintiffs and cross defendants and moving party. Demurrer To Third Amended Cross-Complaint As To The 4th And 6th Causes Of Action; Pos; P/A, Declaration Filed
Off Calendar May-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM NG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE FOR THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST''S Demurrer to Third Amended Cross-Complaint as to the 4th and 6th Causes of Action. Appearances: Gary Lee, Esq. Attorney at Law, (650) 423-8888, appearing for Plaintiffs and cross defendants and moving party. Edward Liu, Attorney at Law, (415) 781-8251, appearing for Defendant and cross complainant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEMURRER OVERRULED. THE COURT CANNOT RULE AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THE CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT CLEARLY DISCLOSE THAT THE CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED AND THE DISCOVERY RULE MAY APPLY IN THIS CASE. FURTHER, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS NOT AVAILABLE TO PLAINTIFF AS TO DEFENDANTS' COUNTERCLAIM IF THE PERIOD HAS NOT RUN ON IT AT THE TIME OF THE COMMENCEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S ACTION EVEN THOUGH IT HAS RUN WHEN THE COUNTERCLAIM IS PLEADED. JONES V. MORTIMER (1946) 28 CAL. 2ND 627, 633. (Defense counsel will prepare Order.) (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2008 Continued To Feb-06-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM NG, individually and as trustee for The 2004 Ng Family Trust's DEMURRER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, AND 10.
APPEARANCES: Gary T. Lee, Esq. 650/423-8888 for pltf/x-deft & moving party; Edward Liu, Esq. 781-8251 for deft/x-complainant & opposing party.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 4TH-8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINT MUST ALLEGE FACTS DEMONSTRATING THAT THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE DECISION IN PARSONS V. TICKNER (1995) 31 CAL. APP. 4TH 1513, 1525 EXPLAINS WHAT IS REQUIRED. IN ADDITION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST DESCRIBE THE ASSETS THAT ALLEGEDLY WERE CONVERTED IN THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION; MUST PLEAD FRAUD WITH GREATER PARTICULARITY; AND MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR ELDER ABUSE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 9TH AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR THE FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT SUPPORT SUCH CLAIMS. In addition, Court allows leave to amend until 12/5/08. Order on demurrer signed in open court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
